free_text: Effect of projectile structure on evaporation residue
Rolled tantalum metal foils of thickness around 1.5
mg/cm2 were backed by 2 mg/cm2 thick aluminium foil to
form a target catcher assembly. A stack of two such
target catcher assemblies was bombarded with the beams
The duration of the bombardment was about 1
hour for 12-C and about 4-9 hours for 13-C.

The duration of the counting was initially 300
seconds followed by successively longer periods of 1000

Three irradiations were carried out for both the
For the variation in the beam intensity during the
irradiation time. The correction factors are
Comparison of the measured excitation functions
with the predictions of the PACE2 code showed a small
but significant cross section for incomplete fusion
(ICF). The fraction of ICF in the total cross section
for complete fusion and ICF was found to be higher in
the case of 12-C than that in the case of 13-C
indicating the influence of the cluster structure
properties of the projectile in ICF reactions.
